闲.origin: 闲 shows a piece of wood 木 inside a door 门, like a bar that keeps you home with nothing to do; it means idle or free.

sentence.en: On weekends I am very free.

闷.origin: 闷 puts a heart 心 behind a door 门, like a heart shut inside; it means stuffy or bored.

sentence.en: It is very stuffy in the room.

闸.origin: 闸 means a floodgate or sluice that opens and closes like a door 门 to control water.

sentence.en: The worker opened the sluice gate.

闽.origin: 闽 is the short name for Fujian province in southeast China.

阀.origin: 阀 means a valve that opens and shuts to control flow; it can also mean a powerful clique.

阅.origin: 阅 means to read or to inspect something carefully.

阉.origin: 阉 means to castrate an animal; it was also used for palace eunuchs.

阎.origin: 阎 once meant a village gate; today it is mostly a family name, as in 阎王, the king of the underworld.

sentence.en: There is the King of Hell in the story.

阔.origin: 阔 means wide and broad, like a big open space.

队.origin: 队 shows people 人 gathered by a mound 阝; it means a team or a line.

防.origin: 防 means to guard against or protect, like building a wall 阝 to keep danger out.

sentence.en: Washing hands can prevent getting sick.

阴.origin: 阴 is the place 阝 of the moon 月; it means shady, cloudy, or the dark side.

阵.origin: 阵 pictures a formation 阝 of war chariots 车; it means a battle array or a short burst.

sentence.en: A gust of wind blew over.

阶.origin: 阶 means stairs or steps cut into a hillside 阝.

阿.origin: 阿 is a friendly prefix put before names and titles, as in 阿姨 (auntie).

陇.origin: 陇 is the short name for the Gansu area and its mountains.

陈.origin: 陈 means to lay out or display; it is also a very common family name.

sentence.en: Toys are displayed in the shop.

降.origin: 降 shows feet coming down a hill 阝; it means to descend or to lower.

限.origin: 限 means a boundary or limit, the line you should not pass.

sentence.en: There is a time limit here.

陕.origin: 陕 is the short name for Shaanxi province in northwest China.

陡.origin: 陡 describes a hillside 阝 that is steep and hard to climb.

险.origin: 险 first meant a narrow mountain pass and now means dangerous or risky.

sentence.en: Be careful of danger when crossing the road.

陪.origin: 陪 means to accompany someone and keep them company.

陶.origin: 陶 means pottery and ceramics shaped from clay.

sentence.en: This piece of pottery is very pretty.

陷.origin: 陷 shows a pit 臽 by a slope 阝; it means to sink in or fall into a trap.

sentence.en: Watch out for the trap on the ground.

隆.origin: 隆 means grand and prosperous, or a deep rumbling sound.

隋.origin: 隋 is the name of the Sui dynasty in Chinese history.

sentence.en: The Sui dynasty was very short.

隐.origin: 隐 means to hide or conceal something out of sight.

sentence.en: The cat hides under the bed.

隔.origin: 隔 means to separate or partition, like a wall between two spaces.

雀.origin: 雀 joins small 小 and bird 隹 to mean a sparrow, a tiny bird.

sentence.en: There is a sparrow in the tree.

雁.origin: 雁 means a wild goose, the bird that flies south in autumn.

雅.origin: 雅 means elegant, graceful, and refined.

雇.origin: 雇 means to hire or employ someone to work.

雌.origin: 雌 means female, used for animals and birds.

雪.origin: 雪 puts rain 雨 on top of a hand sweeping; it means snow, the white winter rain.

雷.origin: 雷 is a storm 雨 over the fields 田; it means thunder.

sentence.en: It is thundering in the sky.

雾.origin: 雾 has rain 雨 on top; it means fog and mist that float in the air.

sentence.en: There is thick fog in the morning.

需.origin: 需 shows rain 雨 that crops wait for; it means to need or require.

震.origin: 震 has rain 雨 above; it means to shake or quake, like the ground in an earthquake.

霉.origin: 霉 means mold and mildew, the fuzz that grows on damp food.

word.en: to grow mold, to go moldy

霍.origin: 霍 pictures birds 隹 startled by a storm 雨; it means quickly or suddenly, and is a family name.

霎.origin: 霎 has rain 雨 on top; it means a brief moment or a light drizzle.

sentence.en: In an instant it began to rain.

霜.origin: 霜 has rain 雨 on top; it means frost, the white ice on cold mornings.

sentence.en: There is frost on the grass.

霞.origin: 霞 has rain 雨 on top; it means the rosy clouds at sunrise and sunset.

霸.origin: 霸 means a tyrant or to dominate by force.

sentence.en: He likes to read stories about the overlord.

靓.origin: 靓 means pretty and good-looking, often used in southern China.

非.origin: 非 shows two wings spread in opposite ways; it means not or wrong.

革.origin: 革 pictures a stretched animal hide; it means leather, and also to change or reform.

sentence.en: These shoes are made of leather.

靴.origin: 靴 has leather 革 on the side; it means boots.

靶.origin: 靶 has leather 革 on the side; it means a target you aim at.

sentence.en: He aims at the target and shoots an arrow.
